LAW AND MOTION, PLAINTIFF ISIDRO MIRANDA'S MOTION TO APPROVE PENALTIES SOUGHT AS PART OF A SETTLEMENT PURSUANT TO THE PRIVATE ATTORNEYS GENERAL ACT (PAGA). PLAINTIFF ISIDRO MIRANDA'S MOTION TO APPROVE PAGA SETTLEMENT IS GRANTED. ALTHOUGH THE MOTION PAPERS DO NOT FULLY ADDRESS ALL OF THE ISSUES RAISED BY THE COURT IN RESPONSE TO THE PREVIOUS MOTION, MOST NOTABLY NO TIME RECORDS ARE PROVIDED TO ENABLE THE COURT TO CALCULATE A LODESTAR, SUFFICIENT INFORMATION HAS BEEN PROVIDED TO WARRANT APPROVING THE SETTLEMENT. ORDER IS SIGNED. (SEE SCANNED ORDER FOR DETAILS) JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, CLERK: M.GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR#6185, TONYVAUGHN@YAHOO.COM, REPORTED. =(302/HK)

CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Dec-05-2018 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Approve Penalties Sought As Part Of Settlement Pursuant To The Private Attorneys General Act (Paga), Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof
Off Calendar Oct-09-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF ISIDRO MIRANDA's Motion To Approve Penalties Sought As Part Of A Settlement Pursuant To The Private Attorneys General Act (Paga).

Plaintiff Isidro Miranda's motion to approve PAGA settlement is granted. Although the motion papers do not fully address all of the issues raised by the court in response to the previous motion, most notably no time records are provided to enable the court to calculate a lodestar, sufficient information has been provided to warrant approving the settlement.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M.Goodman, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR#6185, tonyvaughn@yahoo.com, Reported. =(302/HK)

CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Sep-12-2018 Continued To Dec-11-18 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610
MINI MINUTES:
Hector Martinez, Esq. appearing for the plaintiff. The Order to Show Cause is re-set to DEC-11-2018 at 10:30 AM in Department 610. Judge: TERI L. JACKSON; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Proceedings not reported.

CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jun-26-2018 Continued To Sep-11-18 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Approve Penalties Sought As Part Of A Settlement; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof
Off Calendar May-08-2018 Denied
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF ISIDRO MIRANDA's Motion To Approve Penalties Sought As Part Of A Settlement Pursuant to the Private Attorney General Act ("PAGA").

Plaintiff Isidro Miranda's motion to approve settlement of his PAGA claims is denied without prejudice to Mr. Miranda addressing the deficiencies in the moving papers in a re-filed motion, if he can do so in good faith. The moving papers fail to provide a sufficient quantitative or qualitative analysis of any of the asserted violations of the Labor Code which form the basis of Mr. Miranda's PAGA claims. Nor do the moving papers provide a sufficient explanation why Mr. Miranda is receiving any payment, much less $30,000, for his "individual claims" when he did not allege any individual claims and when he is also receiving a "representative incentive payment." The disproportionate amount of money proposed to be paid to Mr. Miranda suggests that he may not be a proper representative plaintiff for either the absent aggrieved parties or the LWDA. Nor do the moving papers provide a sufficient explanation why $5,000 and not some greater amount is proposed to be paid to the LWDA. Lastly, the moving papers fail to provide any data from which the court can calculate a lodestar as a cross-check on the attorney's fees sought by Mr. Miranda's counsel.

Counsel for Mr. Miranda is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's Ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Gordon Aiavao, CSR#11216, gaiavao@sftc.org, Reported. (D302/HK)

CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Jul-20-2017 Continued To Jan-29-18 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Jan-18-2017 Set For Trial On Oct-02-17 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Apr-21-2017 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES - ATTY. MARCO A. PALOU OF MALLISON & MARTINEZ, TEL. NO. 1-510-832-9999 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. ROMAN OF ZHUK OF FINE, BOGGS & PERKINS LLP, TEL. NO. 1-650-712-8908 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Defendants Anderson Enterprises, Inc. and Andy Hansen's motion for summary judgment is denied. There is a triable issue whether the last date of Mr. Miranda's employment by Anderson Enterprises was February 17, 2102. (Miranda Dec. pars. 5-11). The court liberally construes Mr. Miranda's opposing declaration when reviewing this summary judgment motion. (Corwin v. Los Angeles Newspaper Services Bureau, Inc. (1971) 4 Cal.3d 842, 851-852.) Mr. Miranda's objection to the reply evidence is sustained per San Diego Watercrafts, Inc. v. Wells Fargo Bank, N.A. (2002) 102 Cal.App.4th 308, 316. Mr. Miranda's failure to provide sufficient notice to the LWDA prior to filing this case is not fatal if, as was done here, Mr. Miranda subsequently satisfies the notice requirements and amends the complaint. (Garnett v. ADT, LLC (ED CA 2015) 139 F.Supp.3d 1121, 1126; Caliber Bodyworks, Inc. v. Sup. Crt. (2005) 134 Cal.App.4th 365, 383 n. 18.) Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Carol Karen CSR #8189 =302/hk
